commands_executed: echo "The current user session is blocked by an expired password error. I have attempted several commands (mkdir, ls, id, passwd, chage) to bypass this, but all returned the same error: 'You are required to change your password immediately (password expired)'. Since I do not have a TTY to perform a password change and cannot execute administrative tools to reset the password due to this restriction, I am unable to proceed with the remediation. I am reporting this as a system access issue that requires manual intervention to reset the root/user password."

llm_verdict.message:
The remediation attempt failed because the current user session is blocked by a system-level password expiration policy ("You are required to change your password immediately (password expired)").

Every command attempted—including basic utilities (`id`, `ls`), administrative commands (`mkdir`, `passwd`, `chage`), and even simple `echo` statements—returned the same error: `WARNING: Your password has expired. Password change required but no TTY available.`

Because the session is non-interactive (no TTY) and the operating system is enforcing a password change that requires a TTY, I am unable to execute any further commands to perform the requested remediation. This issue requires manual intervention to reset the user's password or adjust the password aging policy.

per_turn.command: echo "The current user session is blocked by an expired password error. I have attempted several commands (mkdir, ls, id, passwd, chage) to bypass this, but all returned the same error: 'You are required to change your password immediately (password expired)'. Since I do not have a TTY to perform a password change and cannot execute administrative tools to reset the password due to this restriction, I am unable to proceed with the remediation. I am reporting this as a system access issue that requires manual intervention to reset the root/user password."
